name: AOS SDLC Pipeline — End-to-End Template
requirement:
Objective: Design and implement a robust, highly declarative data transformation engine. The initial focus is on processing financial and numerical data, but the core execution framework must be highly extensible to support different data types and processing engines in the future.

Core Technology Stack:

Data Processing: Polars (for high-performance data manipulation).

Validation & Contracts: Pydantic (for type safety and schema validation).

Observability: OpenLineage (for tracking inputs, outputs, and process metadata).

Architectural Requirements:

Declarative Manifests & Contracts: All inputs, outputs, and processes must be strictly defined using configuration manifests and Pydantic-enforced contracts.

Stateless Execution & Clean Code: The processing functions themselves must be entirely stateless. The logic should adhere strictly to Clean Code principles, relying on the manifests to dictate the flow.

DAG Workflow Capabilities: The engine must handle complex states, activities, and processes. It needs to support workflows that branch out (parallel processing/forking) and merge back together (joins/unions).

Pluggable Framework: The execution framework must be flexible enough to swap or add different data-type processing engines without rewriting the core orchestration logic.

Required Data Operations (Initial Phase):

Basic arithmetic (add, subtract, etc.).

Complex financial calculations.

Data aggregation and filtering.

Adding/mutating columns dynamically.

Future Scope (Design with this in mind):

The system will eventually need to ingest and analyze unstructured data, including code repositories, text documents, audio files, images, and videos. The core engine should be agnostic enough to route these to specialized worker engines later on.

Output Request: Please provide a high-level system architecture design, an outline of the core Pydantic models for the Manifest/Contract system, and a basic Python proof-of-concept demonstrating a stateless Polars transformation guided by a declarative contract.

tasks.description: Architecture Vision: decompose the requirement into a PRD and establish TOGAF Phase A boundaries.
Business Architecture: map stakeholder concerns to feature sets and generate BDD scenarios.
Information Systems Architecture: define data contracts (Pydantic models) and API surface.
Technology Architecture: select runtime stack (LiteLLM / LangGraph / Pypes) and infra constraints.
Implementation wave: scaffold modules, write stubs, wire CLI entry points.
Review wave: run quality gates, compile BDD stubs, verify schema conformance.

bdd_scenarios.then: a PRD JSON and Gherkin feature file are emitted to data_out/prd/

bdd_scenarios.given: a manifest with TOGAF phase map and quality gates
bdd_scenarios.then: ADRs are emitted at each TOGAF phase boundary with monotonic sequence numbers

bdd_scenarios.given: a quality gate of kind bdd
bdd_scenarios.when: the gate command exits non-zero with on_failure=halt
bdd_scenarios.then: the wave does not advance and QualityGateError is raised
